title: Credential Compromise with Recent Rolling-Window Activity Surge
narrative: Compromised card credentials are being exploited during a condensed timeframe, creating a spike in rolling-window card counters while individual transactions maintain plausible characteristics. The fraudster executes multiple purchases across diverse merchants within hours or days, causing C8 and C11 metrics to elevate sharply above the cardholder's historical baseline. Each transaction passes basic verification checks—amounts are reasonable, email and address formats validate correctly—but the card's recent usage intensity reveals unauthorized access. This pattern emerges when stolen credentials are shared among fraud rings or sold on carding forums, resulting in sudden multi-merchant activity bursts. The cardholder's genuine spending pattern shows consistent, moderate-velocity usage, but the compromised period exhibits concentrated exploitation before the card is reported or blocked.
indicators: C8 >= 29 (p90), elevated card-merchant count in recent rolling window
C11 >= 27 (p90), high card-transaction count in recent aggregation period
C1 / C11 ratio < 2.5, indicating concentrated recent activity relative to lifetime
C14 >= 7 (p90), elevated unique-day transaction count in rolling window
card1_distinct_addr1 >= 4, multiple shipping addresses in recent timeframe
D1 between 180-730, established device-card relationship excludes first-use scenario
indicator_explanations: The card has been used at an unusually high number of different merchants recently
Transaction frequency in the recent window far exceeds the cardholder's normal pattern
Most of the card's lifetime activity is concentrated in a very recent period
The card shows purchases on multiple different days within a short rolling window
Multiple different shipping destinations appear for this card in recent transactions
The device has prior history with this card, ruling out genuine first-time pairing
summary: Card shows explosive recent activity across many merchants and addresses while individual transaction characteristics remain unremarkable. The rolling-window counters reveal intensity inconsistent with the cardholder's established spending patterns.
reasoning: Legitimate cardholders maintain relatively stable usage patterns over time. A sudden surge in rolling-window metrics—especially across diverse merchants and shipping addresses—indicates the credentials have been compromised and are being exploited rapidly before detection occurs.
